hair.description: Black hair — same role as player family

subcell_fill.description: Yellow sub-cell rendering fill — rendering technique color, not a body part

skin.description: Flesh tone — same role as player family

skin_detail.description: Dark red face detail — used for ^ expression glyph in death pose

shirt_primary.description: Purple torso — same role as player family

pants_dark.description: Dark blue pants — same role as player family

pants_bright.description: Bright blue pants highlight — same role as player family

boots_primary.description: Brown boots — same role as player family

regions.name: hair
regions.notes: Single transition cell with skin fg and hair bg. In death pose, the head is more compact. Glyph 220 (▄) creates a half-block transition between hair (upper) and face (lower).

regions.name: face
regions.notes: Death pose face uses glyph 94 (^) instead of " and v — different expression for death state. The ^ with dark-red fg on skin bg represents a grimace or closed expression. Same skin (#ff5555) and hair (#ffff55) colors as player family.

regions.name: shirt
regions.notes: Death pose spreads the torso horizontally. Arms at (3,7) and (7,7) use glyph 240 (≡) with skin bg — arms splayed out. Shirt center (5,7) shows skin bg bleeding through. Same purple (#aa00aa) as player.

regions.name: pants
regions.notes: Compact pants row. Transition cells at (4,8) and (6,8) blend shirt_primary fg with pants_dark bg. Same blue palette as player.

regions.name: boots
regions.notes: Two boots with a center gap cell. Center cell (5,9) uses pants_dark fg on hair bg — this is a gap/ground cell between the boots, not part of the boots themselves. Same brown (#aa5500) as player.

ambiguities: Death pose frame 0 appears to be the standing/initial death frame — later frames (1-4) likely show collapse animation with different body orientations
The ^ (glyph 94) expression glyph is death-specific — semantically equivalent to the " eyes and v mouth in the player idle pose but representing a different emotional state
Glyph 240 (≡) at arm cells is death-specific — the arm representation changes between families/poses
Center gap cell (5,9) between boots uses pants_dark fg — ambiguous whether this is 'pants bottom' or 'ground fill'; labeled as gap for safety
Shirt center cell (5,7) uses skin bg (#ff5555) instead of shirt bg (#aa00aa) — this may represent skin visible through torn/open shirt in death pose, or it may be a rendering artifact
Only 3 layers (L0-L2) vs 4 in player/attack — no L3 detail layer; plydie may use fewer visual layers by design
Only frame 0 annotated; frames 1-4 of the death animation need separate annotation as body geometry changes significantly during collapse
